new_classes.label_en: Wine with PDO in containers ≤ 2 L with actual alcoholic strength > 13% vol and ≤ 15% vol (CN 2204 22 38)
new_classes.label_de: Wein mit g.U. in Behältnissen ≤ 2 L mit einem tatsächlichen Alkoholgehalt > 13 % vol und ≤ 15 % vol (KN 2204 22 38)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes bearing a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO), presented in containers of a nominal volume not exceeding 2 litres, with an actual alcoholic strength by volume greater than 13% vol and not exceeding 15% vol, as classified under CN subheading 2204 22 38.

coverage_explanation: The legal text for the range 22042222–22042278 specifies: (1) wine of fresh grapes, (2) with a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO), (3) in containers ≤ 2 L. Additional note 6(a) of Chapter 22 (not reproduced in full here) further differentiates sub-codes within this range by actual alcoholic strength bands and total dry extract thresholds. CN 2204 22 38 corresponds to PDO wine in containers ≤ 2 L with actual alcoholic strength > 13% vol and ≤ 15% vol (the intermediate band between 2204 22 26 [≤ 13%] and 2204 22 48 [> 15%, low extract] / 2204 22 38 [> 13% ≤ 15%]). The PDO quality, small container, and alcohol band are captured. The total dry extract sub-criterion (if any for this specific code) and the full text of Additional Note 6(a) are not available in the provided legal notes, reducing coverage. Disjointness with sibling PDO small-container classes (LowAlcohol, HighAlcohol) is asserted via complement restrictions.
